name: Neekerbreekers at the Marsh's Edge
rewards: shell-breaker trophies; the band's first quiet night; the lesson that an unprovisioned band does not last
flavorIntro: A hobbit farmer outside Staddle has had enough. The neekerbreekers have been at the marsh-edge crops for three seasons running — a writhing mass of clicking, biting, fist-sized insects that scatter when you approach and regroup the moment your back is turned. Clear them out, and the farmer will sleep easy. Let the band fail, and they won't.
designNote: Cull encounter / cooking tutorial. The band is contracted to clear a neekerbreeker infestation for a local hobbit farmer. Kill fight: the band must deal enough damage to cull the swarm before time runs out. UNWINNABLE with no food. Tuned with independent member model (beta=0, decouple, sink) and role-matched food defaults (W: Hearthbread, H: Wanderer's Supper, K: Contemplative Tea, C: Ranger's Fare). Validated post-tier-table-fix (T2+ rebuilt for smooth HP/s progression, no cliff at Hearthkeeper→Initiate boundary). Win rates at band Lv1 (unlock floor), 1000 runs: FL1(5.0)=29%, FL2(5.2)=55%, FL3(5.4)=80%, FL4(5.6)=91%. Drain-dominant: every cooking level does visible work across all four Hearthkeeper sub-levels. Stat bonuses also contribute — higher DPS clears the swarm faster, reducing exposure time. IP: 'Neekerbreekers' is Tolkien's coined name — placeholder pending IP review.
